title: Define WorkItem and context type interfaces
description:
Define all TypeScript interfaces for the get-item feature per data-model.md. These types are shared by ALL user stories and must be in place before any service or command implementation.

Interfaces to define:
- WorkItem: id (number), rev (number), title (string), state (string), type (string), assignedTo (string|null), description (string|null), areaPath (string), iterationPath (string), url (string)
- AzdoContext: org (string), project (string)
- AuthCredential: pat (string), source ('env' | 'credential-store' | 'prompt')
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] WorkItem interface defined with all 10 fields and correct types
- [ ] AzdoContext interface defined with org and project fields
- [ ] AuthCredential interface defined with pat and source literal union type
- [ ] All interfaces exported from src/types/work-item.ts
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode passes (no any types)
notes: File: src/types/work-item.ts | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/data-model.md | Can run in parallel with T004.

title: Implement credential store wrapper
description:
Create a wrapper around @napi-rs/keyring for storing and retrieving PATs from the Windows Credential Manager.

API:
- getPat(): Promise<string | null> — retrieve stored PAT (service: 'azdo-cli', account: 'pat')
- storePat(pat: string): Promise<void> — persist PAT to credential manager

Must handle errors gracefully: if credential manager is unavailable (e.g., running on Linux without secret-service), return null from getPat and silently skip storePat rather than crashing. This allows the auth chain to fall through to the interactive prompt.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] getPat() exported from src/services/credential-store.ts
- [ ] storePat() exported from src/services/credential-store.ts
- [ ] Uses @napi-rs/keyring with service 'azdo-cli' and account 'pat'
- [ ] getPat returns null when no credential stored
- [ ] getPat returns stored PAT string when available
- [ ] storePat persists PAT to credential manager
- [ ] Graceful error handling if credential manager unavailable (returns null / no-op)
- [ ] No 'any' types used
notes: File: src/services/credential-store.ts | FR-012 | Can run in parallel with T014. Windows-only initially per clarifications.

title: Install @napi-rs/keyring dependency
description: The get-item command needs Windows Credential Manager integration for PAT persistence. @napi-rs/keyring is the community-accepted successor to the archived keytar package (archived Dec 2022). It uses Rust via napi-rs instead of C++ node-gyp, shipping prebuilt binaries for Windows x64/ARM64, macOS, and Linux — no compilation step needed. Per research.md decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] @napi-rs/keyring added as runtime dependency in package.json
- [ ] npm install succeeds without errors
- [ ] Package is importable in TypeScript with proper type resolution
notes: File: package.json | Run: npm install @napi-rs/keyring | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/research.md

title: Run quickstart end-to-end validation
description:
Execute all scenarios from specs/002-get-item-command/quickstart.md end-to-end to verify the complete feature works:

1. Build the project (npm run build)
2. Test with explicit args: azdo get-item <id> --org <org> --project <project> (with AZDO_PAT set)
3. Test git remote detection: azdo get-item <id> (from Azure DevOps repo, no flags)
4. Test interactive prompt: unset AZDO_PAT, run command, verify prompt appears
5. Test --short flag: verify truncated output
6. Test error cases: invalid ID, bad PAT, non-existent work item
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] npm run build succeeds
- [ ] Explicit args scenario works correctly
- [ ] Git remote auto-detection scenario works
- [ ] Interactive prompt scenario works
- [ ] --short flag produces truncated output
- [ ] Error cases produce correct messages
- [ ] All exit codes correct (0 success, 1 error)
notes: Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/quickstart.md | Requires a real Azure DevOps org/project/work-item for testing.

title: Implement PAT resolution from env variable
description:
Implement the first step of the PAT resolution chain: reading AZDO_PAT from the environment. This is the simplest auth path and enables the MVP flow.

Export resolvePat() that checks process.env.AZDO_PAT. Returns AuthCredential with source 'env' if found, or null if not set. This function will be extended in US3 (T015) to add credential store and interactive prompt fallbacks.

title: Extend PAT resolution to full chain
description:
Extend resolvePat() in auth.ts to implement the complete PAT resolution chain per FR-013:

1. Check AZDO_PAT environment variable → return AuthCredential(source: 'env') if set
2. Check credential store via getPat() → return AuthCredential(source: 'credential-store') if found
3. Prompt interactively via promptForPat() → if user provides PAT, persist via storePat(), return AuthCredential(source: 'prompt')
4. If all three fail → throw error with message: 'Error: Authentication cancelled. Set AZDO_PAT environment variable or run again to enter a PAT.'

This replaces the simple env-var-only version from T005 with the full chain.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] resolvePat() checks env var first (AZDO_PAT)
- [ ] Falls back to credential store (getPat) if env var not set
- [ ] Falls back to interactive prompt if credential store empty
- [ ] Persists PAT to credential store after successful prompt
- [ ] Returns AuthCredential with correct source for each path
- [ ] Throws error with actionable message if all sources fail
- [ ] Error message matches CLI contract (prompt cancelled scenario)
notes: File: src/services/auth.ts | FR-013 | Depends on T009 (command wired), T013 (credential store), T014 (prompt).

title: Create get-item command definition
description:
Create the get-item subcommand using commander.js. This is the core command registration and argument/option parsing.

Command signature: azdo get-item <id> [options]
- Positional arg: id (required, must be positive integer per FR-010)
- Options: --org <org>, --project <project>, --short
- Validation: --org and --project must both be provided or both omitted (FR-009)
- ID validation: must be a positive integer, reject before any API call

On valid input: resolve PAT via resolvePat(), call getWorkItem(), format and display output. On invalid input: write error to stderr, exit 1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] get-item subcommand registered with commander in src/commands/get-item.ts
- [ ] <id> positional argument parsed and validated as positive integer
- [ ] --org and --project options defined as optional strings
- [ ] --short flag defined as boolean
- [ ] Validation error if only --org or only --project provided
- [ ] Validation error if id is not a positive integer
- [ ] Errors written to stderr with exit code 1
- [ ] Success output written to stdout with exit code 0
- [ ] Follows POSIX conventions per constitution Principle I
notes: File: src/commands/get-item.ts | FR-001, FR-002, FR-009, FR-010 | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/contracts/cli-contract.md

title: Implement work item display formatting
description:
Implement the output formatting for work item display in the get-item command handler.

Default mode (full): Display all fields in key-value format:
ID, Type, Title, State, Assigned To, Area, Iteration, URL, then Description (full text).

Short mode (--short): Display core fields only (ID, Type, Title, State, Assigned To, URL) with description truncated to first 3-5 lines followed by ellipsis.

Important: Azure DevOps stores descriptions as HTML. Must strip HTML tags for clean terminal output.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Default mode displays: ID, Type, Title, State, Assigned To, Area, Iteration, URL, Description
- [ ] Fields formatted with aligned labels per CLI contract output format
- [ ] --short mode shows core fields only with truncated description (3-5 lines + ellipsis)
- [ ] HTML tags stripped from description for plain text output
- [ ] Null assignedTo displays as 'Unassigned' or similar
- [ ] Null description handled gracefully (shows nothing or 'No description')
notes: File: src/commands/get-item.ts | FR-004, FR-004a | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/contracts/cli-contract.md (Output section)

title: Install @napi-rs/keyring dependency
Implement Azure DevOps REST API client
description:
Implement a fetch()-based REST client for the Azure DevOps Work Items API v7.1. This is the core HTTP layer used by all user stories.

Function signature: getWorkItem(context: AzdoContext, id: number, pat: string): Promise<WorkItem>

API details:
- Endpoint: GET https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project}/_apis/wit/workitems/{id}?api-version=7.1
- Auth: Basic header with Base64 of ':{pat}' (empty username, PAT as password)
- Response: JSON with id, rev, fields object, _links. Map fields.System.* to WorkItem interface.
- Required PAT scope: vso.work (Work Items read)

Must throw typed/descriptive errors for:
- 401 Unauthorized (invalid/expired PAT)
- 403 Forbidden (insufficient permissions)
- 404 Not Found (work item doesn't exist)
- Network failures (fetch rejects)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] getWorkItem function exported from src/services/azdo-client.ts
- [ ] Uses native fetch() (Node 18+ built-in, no axios/node-fetch)
- [ ] Basic auth header correctly encoded as Base64(':' + pat)
- [ ] API response fields mapped to WorkItem interface
- [ ] 401 response throws auth error with actionable message
- [ ] 403 response throws permission error
- [ ] 404 response throws not-found error
- [ ] Network failure throws connection error
- [ ] No use of 'any' type
notes: File: src/services/azdo-client.ts | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/research.md (Azure DevOps REST API section) | Can run in parallel with T003.

title: Integrate git remote detection into get-item command
description:
Modify the get-item command handler to call detectAzdoContext() when --org and --project are not provided. Surface git-remote errors to stderr per the CLI contract error messages.

Flow change in get-item handler:
1. If --org and --project provided → use them directly (existing US1 path)
2. If neither provided → call detectAzdoContext() to resolve from git remote
3. If detectAzdoContext() throws → write error to stderr, exit 1
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] get-item works without --org/--project when in Azure DevOps repo
- [ ] Falls back to git remote detection when flags omitted
- [ ] Error from detectAzdoContext surfaces to stderr
- [ ] Existing US1 flow (explicit flags) still works unchanged
- [ ] Non-Azure remote error message matches CLI contract
notes: File: src/commands/get-item.ts | FR-005, FR-007, FR-008 | Modifies existing file from US1.

title: Implement interactive PAT prompt with masked input
description:
Implement a password-style prompt that asks the user to paste their Azure DevOps PAT with masked (asterisk) input.

Uses Node.js built-in readline with process.stdin.setRawMode(true) for hidden input — zero external dependencies per constitution Principle V.

Implementation:
1. Check process.stdin.isTTY — return null if not a TTY (piped/non-interactive)
2. Display 'Enter your Azure DevOps PAT: '
3. Read characters one at a time in raw mode, echoing '*' for each
4. Handle special keys: Enter (submit), Ctrl+C (cancel), Backspace (delete last)
5. On Enter: restore normal mode, return accumulated string
6. On Ctrl+C: restore normal mode, return null
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] promptForPat() exported from src/services/auth.ts
- [ ] Displays 'Enter your Azure DevOps PAT: ' prompt
- [ ] Input characters masked with asterisks
- [ ] Enter key submits the PAT
- [ ] Ctrl+C cancels and returns null
- [ ] Backspace deletes last character
- [ ] Returns null if not a TTY (process.stdin.isTTY check)
- [ ] Uses only Node.js built-in readline (no external deps)
- [ ] Restores terminal to normal mode after prompt
notes: File: src/services/auth.ts | FR-011 | Can run in parallel with T013. ~25 lines of code per research.md.

title: Implement git remote reader
description:
Implement detectAzdoContext(): AzdoContext that reads the origin remote URL from git and passes it through parseAzdoRemote.

Steps:
1. Run 'git remote get-url origin' via child_process.execSync
2. Parse the URL with parseAzdoRemote()
3. Return AzdoContext on success

Error cases:
- Not in a git repo → throw error: 'Not in a git repository. Provide --org and --project explicitly.' (FR-008)
- Remote is not Azure DevOps → throw error: 'Git remote "origin" is not an Azure DevOps URL. Provide --org and --project explicitly.' (FR-007)
- Origin remote doesn't exist → throw error similar to not-in-repo
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] detectAzdoContext exported from src/services/git-remote.ts
- [ ] Reads origin remote URL via child_process.execSync
- [ ] Passes URL to parseAzdoRemote for extraction
- [ ] Throws descriptive error when not in a git repo
- [ ] Throws descriptive error when remote is not Azure DevOps
- [ ] Trims whitespace from git output
notes: File: src/services/git-remote.ts | FR-007, FR-008 | Uses origin remote by default per assumptions.

title: Implement CLI error handling
description:
Add comprehensive error handling to the get-item command. Catch errors from azdo-client and auth services, map them to user-friendly stderr messages per the CLI contract error table.

Error messages must be actionable — tell the user what went wrong AND how to fix it.

Error mapping:
- Invalid ID → 'Error: Work item ID must be a positive integer. Got: "{input}"'
- Missing org/project pair → 'Error: --org and --project must both be provided, or both omitted.'
- Auth failure (401) → 'Error: Authentication failed. Check that your PAT is valid and has the "Work Items (read)" scope.'
- Not found (404) → 'Error: Work item {id} not found in {org}/{project}.'
- Permission denied (403) → 'Error: Access denied. Your PAT may lack permissions for project "{project}".'
- Network error → 'Error: Could not connect to Azure DevOps. Check your network connection.'
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All error scenarios from CLI contract handled
- [ ] Errors written to stderr (not stdout)
- [ ] Exit code 1 on any error
- [ ] Error messages are actionable (include fix instructions)
- [ ] No stack traces shown to user (catch and format)
- [ ] Error messages match CLI contract exactly
notes: File: src/commands/get-item.ts | FR-014 | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/contracts/cli-contract.md (Error Output section)
